taskDescription: Close the remaining implementation gaps identified in issues #10/#24 (synthetic data) and #11/#25 (SFT training pipeline) so the project can start fine-tuning. The approved plan is at /home/danny/.claude/plans/elegant-floating-russell.md. Key constraint: base model is metric/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b-bf16/transformers/default, lora_r <= 32, answers use \boxed{} format, SFTExample schema is frozen in src/contracts.py:97.

features.description: T1-AB: Integrate configs/synthetic_prompts.yaml into src/data/synthetic.py generation pipeline AND add solver-confidence gate to QualityFilter. (1) Add prompt_config_path: Path | None = None to SyntheticConfig dataclass. (2) Load YAML at runtime with yaml.safe_load and pass prompt_templates dict to generate_from_retry_candidates(). (3) In the generation loop use prompt_templates[category]['system_prompt'] + problem_formulation to build the LLM teacher prompt. (4) Pull dry_run_token_estimate from YAML per-category instead of gold_text_len//4+200. (5) Add solver_confidence_threshold: float = 0.0 to SyntheticConfig. (6) In QualityFilter.accept() after the provenance check add: if self._config.solver_confidence_threshold > 0: conf = ex.provenance.get('solver_confidence', 0.0); if conf < self._config.solver_confidence_threshold: return False, 'low_solver_confidence'. (7) Add one new test to tests/data/test_synthetic.py: set solver_confidence_threshold=0.9 and verify a low-confidence example is rejected. All existing tests must still pass.

T1-D: Create docs/architecture/SYNTHETIC_PROVENANCE.md documenting: artifact layout (data/synthetic/<run_id>.jsonl + .sha256 sidecar), provenance dict schema with fields teacher/generated_at/source_run_id/solver_confidence, fingerprint algorithm (SHA-256 of JSONL bytes), and step-by-step instructions to re-verify a dataset from scratch. Keep it concise (~80 lines max).

T2-A: Create three training config YAML files. (1) configs/lora_baseline.yaml: base_model: metric/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b-bf16/transformers/default, lora_r: 32, lora_alpha: 64, lora_dropout: 0.05, target_modules: [in_proj, out_proj, up_proj, down_proj], load_in_4bit: false, torch_dtype: bfloat16, trust_remote_code: true. (2) configs/lora_qlora.yaml: same but load_in_4bit: true and lora_r: 16. (3) configs/smoke_sft.yaml: extends: configs/lora_baseline.yaml, max_steps: 100, per_device_train_batch_size: 1, dataset_max_rows: 1000, save_steps: 50, gradient_accumulation_steps: 4. These must match the frozen constraints in docs/architecture/COMPETITION.md and docs/execution/plans/issue-25-hpc-queue-runbook.md.

T2-B: Implement src/training/sft_trainer.py (new file, ~80 lines) with function apply_loss_mask(input_ids: list[int], labels: list[int], tokenizer, mask_role: str = 'user') -> list[int] that sets prompt/user/system turn token labels to -100 and leaves assistant turn labels unchanged. Must use type annotations, handle edge case where no assistant turn exists (raise ValueError). Also create src/training/__init__.py. Then create tests/training/__init__.py and tests/training/test_masking.py with 4 tests: test_user_tokens_masked (all user turn tokens have label -100), test_assistant_tokens_unmasked (assistant labels equal input_ids), test_system_tokens_masked (system tokens get -100), test_all_masked_raises (ValueError when no assistant turn). Use a minimal mock tokenizer that returns predictable token boundaries.

T1-C: Complete notebooks/08_synthetic_data_recipe.ipynb beyond its current 14-cell scaffold. The notebook exists but lacks executable generation content. Read the current notebook first. Then update: Cell [7] bind llm_teacher_fn with os.environ.get('LLM_API_KEY') guard — if absent use a stub lambda that returns '\boxed{STUB}'. Cell [9] add actual generation loop calling generate_from_retry_candidates(candidates, config, smoke=True) from src.data.synthetic. Cell [10] fingerprint verification using Path(output_path + '.sha256').read_text(). Cell [11] summary table showing rows generated/rejected/cost/fingerprint. Strip all cell outputs before saving (existing repo convention — use nbstripout or clear outputs manually in the JSON).

T2-C: Create scripts/hpc/ directory with all 8 HPC scripts. Read docs/execution/plans/issue-25-hpc-queue-runbook.md first for full spec. (1) preflight.sh: bash script checking CUDA available, model weights path exists, dataset .sha256 valid, disk space >50GB, required Python packages importable. Exit 0 on pass, 1 on fail with clear error messages. (2) tokenize_dataset.py: load JSONL of SFTExample rows, apply chat template, call apply_loss_mask() from src.training.sft_trainer, save tokenized .pt shards, accept --input/--output/--config args. (3) submit_sft.sbatch: SLURM job (4 GPUs, 64GB RAM, 24h), activates venv, runs tokenize_dataset.py then a training loop stub. (4) submit_rl.sbatch: similar SLURM job for RL phase. (5) checkpoint_policy.py: given a checkpoint dir writes sidecar files trainer_state.json/run_config.json/metrics.jsonl/git_sha.txt/dataset_fingerprint.txt and enforces keep-last-3-plus-best policy. (6) regression_gate.py: loads EvalRecord JSONL + golden SplitArtifactRow JSONL, calls run_gate() from src.evaluation.golden_gate (already implemented), exits 0 on pass / 1 on fail — accept --eval-records and --golden args. (7) package_adapter.py: thin wrapper around src.inference.submission (already implemented), accepts --adapter-dir/--output-dir/--base-model-id/--adapter-rank args. (8) resume_from_latest.py: scans checkpoint dir for highest step number, prints the --resume_from_checkpoint path to stdout. All scripts must have --help, type annotations where Python, and shebang lines.
